step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: The first step of our method is to describe the EM map as a weighted graph $G(V,E)$ . The vertices $v\in V$ are the grid points of the EM map, and the edges $e\in E$ are the 26 direct neighbors in the grid. The weight $w_{i,j}$ ... is defined as the inverse of the product of the map density ... $w_{i,j}=(d_{i}\times d_{j})^{\bar{-}1}$ .
results in a grid of $80\times74\times76$ grid points with a spacing of $1.32\mathring{\mathrm{A}}$ .

evidence.quote: We use the Kruskal algorithm [20] to build this tree from a graph $G$ built on the EM density.
20. Kruskal, J.B. On the shortest spanning subtree of a graph and the traveling salesman problem. Proc. Am. Math. Soc. 1956, 7, 48–50.

evidence.quote: We used the Raptorx ContactMap server [25] to predict the contacts. ... used a cutoff of 0.5 ... ending up with 658, 343, and 563 predicted contacts for chains A, B, and C, respectively.
25. Wang, S.; Sun, S.; Li, Z.; Zhang, R.; Xu, J. Accurate De Novo Prediction of Protein Contact Map by Ultra-Deep Learning Model. PLoS Comput. Biol. 2017, 13, e1005324.

This part of the procedure is based on the map_align algorithm developed by Ovchinnikov et al. [21]. ... use a distance cutoff of $8\mathring{\mathrm{A}}$ between $C\alpha$ atoms. ... used a probability threshold of 0.5
21. Ovchinnikov, S.; ... Protein structure determination using metagenome sequence data. Science 2017, 355, 294–298.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: we developed an algorithm that iteratively concatenates the fragments ... Dynamic programming allowed us to find the subset of fragments ... the algorithm found 18 fragments (Figure 2c) with an average accuracy of $1.2\mathring\mathrm{A}$
Chains A, B, and C were detected automatically without any prior segmentation of the EM density map.

evidence.quote: In the final step, we use Modeller [23] to obtain a full-atom model of the structure.
23. Eswar, N.; ... Comparative protein structure modeling using Modeller. Curr. Protoc. Bioinform. 2006, 15, 5–6.

The obtained model is further refined in the EM map by using the real_space_refine tool [24] from the Phenix suite. ... secondary structures are optimized by using an optional feature in Phenix that annotates secondary structure based on $C\alpha$ positions.
24. Afonine, P.V.; ... New tool: Phenix real space refine. Comput. Crystallogr. Newsl. 2013, 4, 43–44.
